element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player begins with standard weapon before choosing loadout

Players choose between Striker (vertical options), Reverse shot (diagonal rear fire), Six Way Shot (semi-auto omnidirectional), Homing Shot (tracking), Red Rockets (straight), Green Homing Missiles, or Border shield
Adds vertical firepower alignment, requires positioning awareness
Enables covering approach threats and retreat scenarios
Reduces aiming precision requirement, trades control for automation
Collision protection for foreground object encounters
Progressive firepower enhancement through enemy destruction
Scaling challenge across six stages toward mechanical fortress

typical_relief_triggers: Safe scrolling segments with minimal threats
typical_tension_triggers: Foreground obstacle navigation requiring Border shield or precise dodging
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Horizontally scrolling progression with escalating difficulty; reviews noted 'too easy' overall difficulty suggesting forgiving tension curves in early-mid stages, building toward boss encounters

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible entry point; initial stage introduces weapon selection and basic enemy patterns; described as 'too easy' by reviewers
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Gradual escalation across stages 2-4; enemy density and complexity increase; parallax scrolling effects maintain visual engagement
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stages 5-6 approach climactic mechanical fortress encounter; boss patterns likely more complex; final challenge emphasizes survival and weapon choice mastery
notable_spikes: Stage boss encounters at end of each stage
Mechanical fortress final approach and combat sequence

notable_level_patterns.description: Game structures horizontally scrolling content across exactly six stages, each concluding with a boss encounter. Player advances toward orbiting mechanical fortress as narrative and spatial objective.

Each stage remains navigable with any of seven selectable weapons, but stage design implicitly favors certain loadouts. Reverse shot aids escaping threats; Homing Shot reduces precision demand; Striker enables vertical coverage; Border shield protects against foreground collisions.

Stages employ parallax effects to establish visual depth and motion sensation. Genesis port maintains arcade parallax; PC Engine Stage 2 features unique track, suggesting thematic variation rather than structural mechanic change.
